content: Mobile PWA touch event performance pitfalls: (1) Chrome 56+ makes touch/touchmove passive by default on document - preventDefault silently ignored. Must set {passive:false} explicitly. (2) Touchmove handlers doing heavy work block scrolling - use requestAnimationFrame batching. (3) INP (Interaction to Next Paint) is now a Core Web Vitals metric - slow touch handlers directly impact it. (4) Passive listeners let browser scroll immediately without waiting for JS - eliminates scroll jank but loses preventDefault. (5) For game touch areas: set touch-action:none via CSS and use {passive:false} in JS for full control. (6) requestAnimationFrame pattern: set pending flag, check in handler, process in rAF callback.
